description_long: This object is a long, flat expanse of rocky terrain, composed of a dense layer of irregularly shaped stones and gravel tightly packed together. The surface is uneven, with a mottled pattern of grays and muted earth tones, suggesting a natural or minimally maintained rocky field. The edges are straight and parallel, giving the impression of a deliberately defined strip, possibly used as a pathway or border in an urban landscape. The texture is coarse and rugged, with visible variations in stone size and placement, creating a tactile, non-uniform appearance. There are no visible signs of vegetation or soil, and the field appears to be solid and continuous throughout its length. The overall impression is of a durable, hard-wearing surface suitable for supporting foot traffic or demarcating space in parks, plazas, or roadside medians.
description: A long, flat strip of densely packed, irregular gray stones forming a rugged, hard surface. The rocky field is straight-edged and coarse, ideal for urban pathways or borders.
description_view_0: A head-on view showing the narrow width and dense, uneven rocky surface.
description_view_1: A side view highlighting the long, flat expanse and the thin profile of the rocky field.
description_view_2: A rear view similar to the front, displaying the straight edge and consistent rocky texture.
description_view_3: A side view from the opposite direction, again emphasizing the length and flatness of the rocky field.
attribute_location_within_the_urban_envi: Typically found in parks, roadside medians, or as edging in plazas and courtyards.
